_note: AUTHORITATIVE mutable current-state source. The ONLY place current operational state is defined. Mutate via `tools/project-state.py transition`; validate via `tools/project-state.py validate`; generated status blocks are rendered from here via `tools/project-state.py render`. Do not duplicate current-state wording elsewhere outside generated blocks.

delivery_governance.branch_strategy: one complete Phase per branch + one PR (phase/<name>-<purpose>); governance-only uses governance/<purpose>; no direct commits to the protected default branch without explicit PO authorization
delivery_governance.merge_gate: PR merge requires the GitHub Actions 'Project Governance' check (job 'governance') to pass; local validator output alone is insufficient (GH-MANDATORY-CI)

PART B partially COMPLETE (build/vet/gofmt/go test -race all GREEN in Docker golang:1.25): §10 cryptographic SHA-256 advisory lock key (namespace stayconnect:pms-interface-owner:v1 || tenant16||site16||interface16, first 8 bytes big-endian signed bigint) with fixed numeric test vectors + malformed-UUID rejection; §14 CLOSED typed error-code vocabulary (DB_UNAVAILABLE/LOCK_*/REVISION_*/SECRET_*/DIAL_*/PROTOCOL_*/RUNTIME_GENERATION_STALE/QUEUE_OVERFLOW/...) with redacting local-diagnostic boundary + canary log tests (password/room/guest/reservation/folio/endpoint never leak into codes); §17 bounded event queue (fixed capacity, back-pressure timeout, QUEUE_OVERFLOW error + overflow->resync latch, no silent drop, context/close drain, zero goroutines). Follow-up hardening (continuation directive §1-§5) DONE + race-green: linearizable Close/Enqueue queue (RWMutex; 100+ concurrent producers + concurrent Close + accounting, no accept-after-close, no leak); TYPED normalized protocol Event (provenance ids + record type + verified external identity + parsed/normalized timestamps + typed Stay/Guest/Folio fields; raw STX/ETX frame bytes never leave the adapter); debug-logging PII gap closed (logs carry only Code + interface UUID + generation + closed-set stage + counters; no raw/redacted err text; canary tests inspect captured logCoded output); migration APPLY role needs only SELECT+INSERT (live-site apply role proven to hold no UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE/REFERENCES/TRIGGER + no public CREATE), rollback/admin separate; bootstrap rejects arbitrary --target-kind. Part-A gate now 121/121. PART B COORDINATED REWRITE COMPLETE + verified (build/vet/gofmt/go test -race all GREEN in Docker golang:1.25): §6 assignment-scoped identity (Deps.LoadAssignment via Ed25519-verified signed appliance assignment; ListActiveInterfaces(ctx,tenant,site); unassigned=zero work; cross-scope rejected; assignment rotation drains+re-scopes); §7 typed Revision.Validate (explicit connector-kind/endpoint/timezone/normalization/read-only/timeouts/heartbeat/freshness/resync; read-only never defaults true) + SecretGeneration + secret load+AES-256-GCM decrypt ONLY after lock (lock loser never decrypts/dials); rotation reconnect; §8 atomic PG AllocateRuntimeGeneration (stored+1, pins revision+secret) + every axis write EXACT compare-and-set (WHERE runtime_generation=expected, not >=); stale owner->RUNTIME_GENERATION_STALE->close transport; §9 independent-axis UpdateTransport/UpdateContinuity/UpdateSync (heartbeat preserves continuity+sync; worker context, no context.Background; persistent DB error closes transport); §10 explicit DB ownership (one shared owned pool, dedicated connection per advisory lock, Repo.Close, lock-watcher stop, per-ownership queue close, supervisor drain); §11 real production-capable injectable FIAS adapter (errLiveDialDeferred REMOVED; reuses internal/pms framing via exported ReadFramedRecord/WriteFramedRecord/ParseFields/record builders -- no second parser; net.Pipe/injected-dialer tests; LS/LD/LR/LA/GI/GC/GO/DR/DS-DE/LE); §12 outbound read-only allowlist at the guardedConn socket-write CHOKEPOINT (PS/PA/unknown write ZERO bytes to the peer; pmsd imports no P#/Posting/Settlement/Payment path); §13 bounded TYPED Event delivery (invalid event rejected before capacity; overflow->GAP_DETECTED+RESYNC_REQUIRED + DR resync request, no silent drop). PART C COMPLETE: disposable PG16 pmsd integration (assignment-scoped discovery, cross-scope rejection, real pg_try_advisory_lock competition, atomic generation N->N+1, stale-owner CAS rejection, independent-axis preservation, zero svc_* iam_v2 grants) via scripts/pmsd-pg-integration.sh; protocol fake-server tests over net.Pipe (startup handshake, typed domain events carrying NO raw STX/ETX bytes + keyed-HMAC evidence, DS/DE resync, write-chokepoint no-PS, queue-overflow->DR) run with -race; mandatory Software CI .github/workflows/phase3-software.yml (gofmt+build+vet+test+-race + migration-0010 gate + PG16 integration; setup failures FAIL not skip). Part-A gate 121/121; PG16 integration 6/6. INCREMENT 3 [HISTORICAL -- this reopened FIAS/ownership hardening has since COMPLETED; see CURRENT AUTHORITATIVE STATUS at the top]: IMPLEMENTED, ACCEPTANCE WAS REOPENED for authoritative FIAS + ownership hardening (PO reopened after ffb9f0d: FIAS field map was inverted (RN is ROOM, G# is RESERVATION, GN/GF last/first, GA/GD arrival/departure); connector credential model fabricated an unused Secret for a no-auth Protel transport; event admission was in-memory-before-ownership-CAS; keepalive/idle-LA + DR/DS..DE isolated resync + gap/resync barrier + canonical assignment reuse + lock-loss-before-dial + owner-bound AEAD AAD + atomic config snapshot all require correction). Prior CI (Governance 29684187770 / Software 29684187743 on ffb9f0d) is evidence of the PRIOR test set only, NOT final acceptance. HARDENING PROGRESS (event semantics FINALIZED, race-green in Docker golang:1.25): authoritative FIAS field map (RN=room,G#=reservation,GN/GF last/first,GA/GD arrival/departure) DONE. CONNECTOR-OWNED STAY IDENTITY REMOVED: the unsafe LogicalStayKey is gone from the Event contract and from Event.Validate() as a mandatory identity (a connector cannot own Stay lifecycle -- arrival evidence is correctable within one Stay and the connector has no authoritative lifecycle_version). The remaining resolution hint is renamed StayResolutionCandidate and documented NON-AUTHORITATIVE: not unique, never a database identity, never a lifecycle/Folio/Room decision, never sufficient to apply a Stay mutation (Increment 4 resolves Stay/lifecycle_version transactionally); a valid Event may carry an empty candidate. COMPLETE-RECORD FINGERPRINT: SourceEventFingerprint = dedicated keyed-HMAC (purpose PMS_EVENT_IDENTITY, distinct from evidence/encryption keys; empty key fails closed) over the CANONICAL complete parsed record -- every field code+value in wire order, length-prefixed with boundary separators (no field-boundary ambiguity; duplicate occurrences distinguishable; present-but-empty is not absent), plus record type, interface, normalization version, protocol profile and verified sequence, excluding only STX/ETX framing; exact retransmission is idempotent, any token change is distinct, unknown fields influence the fingerprint but not the typed domain model, and no raw record/PII is logged or persisted outside the keyed boundary. NO SILENT TRUNCATION: RN/G#/GN/GF/FO/GA/GD are carried untruncated and an overlong/control-byte value REJECTS the record (never a shortened, possibly-colliding value reaches the queue/inbox); a rejected record drives continuity to GAP_DETECTED and sync to RESYNC_REQUIRED via the new AxisSink.OnContinuityFault (both axes PERSISTED under the pinned runtime generation -- the previously ignored UpdateContinuity/UpdateSync results are no longer discarded; a DB/generation failure closes the transport instead of continuing over an unrecorded gap) and issues a read-only DR resync request, so the Serve loop never silently continues on a malformed record. Timestamp semantics retained (ArrivalRaw=GA/DepartureRaw=GD are Stay dates, ReceivedAt is the local receipt clock, PMSEventAt only from a verified FIAS event-timestamp field -- never GA; ReceivedAt implies no ordering). Tests added/updated + green: complete-record ordered-pair fingerprint (duplicate/distinct/present-vs-absent/retransmit-idempotent), StayResolutionCandidate non-authoritative (Room Move + arrival-correction keep the same candidate, different reservation differs, candidate is not the fingerprint, empty candidate still validates), per-field overlong rejection with no truncated value reaching the queue, and a Serve-boundary malformed-record test proving OnContinuityFault + DR with zero admitted events. CI-stability: a benign MEASUREMENT race in the pre-existing linearizable-close test (TestQueue_LinearizableClose_NoAcceptAfterClose) was corrected -- the close boundary is now observed BEFORE each Enqueue call (an Enqueue that STARTS after Close() returns must never be accepted), removing a false positive where an Enqueue committed while Close held the write lock and was only observed afterward; race-green x30 in Docker golang:1.25. STRICT INGESTION + ATOMIC GAP STATE + SERIALIZED WRITER (directive items 1-6) DONE + race-green (Docker golang:1.25) + PG16-green: (1) ONE strict FIAS record parser (ParsedRecord) replaces the permissive field parser for ingestion -- exact supported record id, normal trailing pipe accepted, internal-empty (||)/one-char/malformed-code/control-byte tokens rejected with a typed ErrRecordMalformed (never silent skipping), order + duplicates + present-but-empty + unknown-valid codes preserved for fingerprinting; a malformed token drives GAP_DETECTED + RESYNC_REQUIRED and admits zero events. (2) Ambiguous duplicate typed fields fail closed (RN and G# exactly once; GN/GF/FO/GA/GD at most once; no first/last-wins, no map collapse) while unknown duplicates stay fingerprint-only. (3) Derived GuestName removed from the connector Event (original first/last retained; display is an Increment-4/presentation concern, so a valid record never fails because two individually-valid names concatenate past a single-field bound). (4) Gap/resync is now ONE transactional repository operation MarkGapAndRequireResync (both axes or neither, exact runtime_generation CAS, resync_started_at reset for pir_resync_coherent, bounded typed reason persisted in last_sync_failure_code, ErrStaleGeneration on ownership change) -- PG16-proven both-together / forced-failure-rolls-back-both / stale-changes-neither / heartbeat-cannot-clear / normal-writes-preserve; the previously-split UpdateContinuity+UpdateSync gap writes are gone. (5) Comments/docs corrected to the strict complete-record keyed-HMAC (no stale reservation/room/date-only wording). (6) Item F: ONE serialized protocol writer owns EVERY outbound frame (LS/LD/LR startup, LS-ack LA, incoming-LA->LA, idle-LA keepalive, DR, LE); no read-loop/timer/worker touches the socket writer, the request channel is bounded, any activity resets the idle timer, cancellation emits a controlled LE and leaks no goroutine, and a write failure ends the ownership cycle -- race-proven concurrent-requests-serialized + startup/acks + >=3 idle keepalives + link-alive-while-idle + cancellation-no-leak + write-failure-terminates. F INTEGRATION HARDENING (control-frame validation + writer shutdown, this directive items 1-4) DONE + race-green: (1) EVERY inbound frame is STRICT-parsed BEFORE any action (idle reset, heartbeat, ack, resync, dispatch): a malformed/unsupported CONTROL record -- or an unknown record id; the speculative HB record is removed and LE is now a recognized control record -- terminates the ownership cycle with the bounded PROTOCOL_FRAMING_ERROR code and records no heartbeat, no ack and no idle reset, while a malformed DOMAIN record drives the atomic gap/resync, admits zero events, requests DR and keeps the barrier active. (2) Cancellation shutdown is prompt and bounded: the sole writer emits a controlled LE within the write deadline then CLOSES the transport to release a reader parked behind a multi-minute heartbeat timeout, so Serve returns within a short bound independent of HeartbeatTimeout, with no goroutine leak (proven with HeartbeatTimeout set to minutes). (3) The writer exposes context-aware Submit(ctx)/SubmitSync(ctx); all critical frames (startup LS/LD/LR, LS/LA acks, DR) are written SYNCHRONOUSLY so a write failure is observed immediately, the request channel stays bounded, and no caller blocks indefinitely behind a stalled socket. (4) Post-start writer-failure coverage proves each of the LS-ack LA, LA-ack LA, idle-keepalive LA and DR writes (and a failed shutdown LE) terminates Serve, closes the transport, releases the reader and leaks no goroutine. G DATA MODEL (durable resync inbox, reuse stay_events; Migration 0010 amended, undeployed) DONE + lifecycle-gate 121/121 + PG16-green: stay_events reused as the append-first durable inbox with additive immutable columns admission_kind (LIVE|RESYNC), admission_runtime_generation, resync_generation (0 for LIVE, >0 for RESYNC), fingerprint_key_version; a se_admission_coherent CHECK; and admission-aware idempotency -- the baseline UNIQUE(scope,external_event_identity) is replaced by TWO partial unique indexes (LIVE dedup per interface; RESYNC dedup per resync generation), so a fresh full roster can restage a record whose content matches a LIVE row. pms_interface_runtime gains resync_generation_seq (monotonic typed-resync allocator, bumped per DS) and published_resync_generation (the ATOMIC single-row PUBLICATION boundary advanced only at a valid DE) with a pir_resync_generation_coherent CHECK (0 <= published <= seq). RESYNC Event rows are IMMUTABLE: activation is NOT a mass row update -- a staged generation becomes consumable only when the one runtime-row boundary reaches its resync_generation, and a partial/failed generation (no DE) leaves the boundary unchanged so its rows stay isolated for deterministic cleanup. The append-only trigger extends its immutable-column set to the admission columns. up/down/reapply + rollback-catalog-equality + owner-parity + zero-service-grant all pass on disposable PG16; a new resync table was NOT needed (stay_events proven sufficient). G PERSISTENCE LAYER DONE + PG16-green + race-green: Repo.AllocateResyncGeneration (monotonic seq bump under exact runtime-generation CAS), AdmitLiveEvent + StageResyncEvent (append-first INSERT inside ONE tx that first PROVES ownership of the exact runtime generation via SELECT..FOR UPDATE on the runtime row, exposing only the durable row id), and PublishResyncGeneration (advance published_resync_generation in ONE atomic row update + mark IN_SYNC/CONTINUOUS, over-publish rejected, CAS-guarded). New PG16 integration tests prove monotonic allocation, staged-invisible-before-publication, atomic-publication-makes-consumable, over-publish-rejected, stale-owner-does-nothing, LIVE-vs-RESYNC partial-unique idempotency, and admission-column immutability. G STATE MACHINE + H APPLICATION BARRIER + DURABLE LIVE ADMISSION DONE + race-green + PG16-green: the worker sink now enforces the barrier and drives the full DS..DE resync lifecycle. RequireInitialResync raises the barrier at connect (sync->RESYNC_REQUIRED) and the adapter sends the INITIAL DR through the single serialized writer; DS (OnResyncStart) allocates a NEW typed resync generation under the exact runtime-generation CAS and opens the staging window; every GI/GC/GO inside the window is StageResyncEvent (immutable RESYNC row, invisible to the Stay engine); DE (OnResyncComplete) atomically PublishResyncGeneration (ONE runtime-row boundary update + IN_SYNC/CONTINUOUS) and only THEN lowers the barrier -- a DE without a DS, or a fault/disconnect before DE, publishes nothing and keeps the barrier up. While the barrier is up (not synced, no window) LIVE records are HELD (zero admission; heartbeat/LA cannot lower it). Once synced, OnDomainEvent does durable AdmitLiveEvent (append-first INSERT under a synchronous ownership proof via SELECT..FOR UPDATE on the runtime row + exact runtime-generation CAS) then records feed continuity; a stale owner admits/stages nothing. In-memory-queue overflow no longer forces a gap -- the durable stay_events row is the AUTHORITATIVE store and the bounded queue is only a benign best-effort wakeup. Sink tests (fakeRepo): held-before-publish, staged-in-window, heartbeat-cannot-lower-barrier, valid-DE-publishes-then-live, no-publish-without-DE, DE-without-DS-is-noop, stale-owner-admits-nothing; adapter test: initial-DR-after-connect; plus a PG16 end-to-end sink lifecycle (held->staged->published->consumable->live) through the real append-only triggers. CI-stability: fixed a pre-existing concurrency bug in internal/localkeys.CreateKeyIfAbsent -- a losing concurrent bootstrapper read the winner mid-write O_EXCL empty file and wrongly failed too-short instead of falling through to the race-safe create; 300x race-stress (Docker golang:1.25, GOMAXPROCS=2) green. CREDENTIAL_MODE (truthful NONE) + MIGRATION-0010 CREDENTIAL-AWARE PIN COHERENCE DONE + race-green + lifecycle-gate 121/121 + PG16-green: Revision carries an EXPLICIT credential_mode (NONE|AUTH_KEY); rev.Validate requires it explicit and fails closed -- a NONE connector must carry NO secret generation (no fabricated secret for the no-auth Protel FIAS transport) and an AUTH_KEY connector must carry one. The worker loads/validates/decrypts a Secret Generation ONLY for AUTH_KEY; a NONE connector dials the no-auth transport with no secret material (spy-proven: dial without decrypt). AllocateRuntimeGeneration pins credential_mode (fail-closed default AUTH_KEY when unspecified); LoadInterface parses config->auth->>credential_mode (fail-closed default AUTH_KEY). Migration 0010 adds pms_interface_runtime.credential_mode and makes pir_connected_pins CREDENTIAL-AWARE: CONNECTED requires a pinned Secret Generation ONLY for AUTH_KEY (a NONE row is legitimately CONNECTED with none). PG16-proven: NONE CONNECTED-without-secret allowed; AUTH_KEY CONNECTED-without-secret rejected by the CHECK. OWNER-BOUND AES-GCM AAD DONE + race-green: the secret decryptor now binds every ciphertext to its EXACT owner (tenant/site/interface/secret-generation) via length-prefixed AES-GCM AAD (purpose pms-secret-aead:v1) -- a ciphertext provisioned for a different interface or generation, or the previously-used nil AAD, fails authentication (unit-proven; the provisioning/encrypt side must use the identical AAD). The remaining §9-§16 items were already present + tested: canonical assignment loader/trust-registry reuse (FileAssignmentLoader + Ed25519-verified LoadAssignment), assignment rotation drain/re-scope (supervisor reconcile loop), fail-closed assignment invalidation, lock-backend-loss handling (locker.Lost -> cancel serve + close socket; a lock loser never dials), panic cleanup + one bounded replacement (supervisor recover -> PANIC_RECOVERED), and startup fail-closed validation (Revision.Validate + env). INCREMENT 3 CONNECTOR HARDENING COMPLETE + race-green + PG16-green + lifecycle-gate 121/121 (both CIs green). INCREMENT 4 STARTED: new package data-plane/internal/stayengine with the DETERMINISTIC, idempotent Stay-resolution DECISION CORE (Resolve maps a durable inbox event + current Stay state to an authoritative Op: CREATE_STAY / UPDATE_STAY / ROOM_MOVE / CHECKOUT / REINSTATE(one lifecycle_version bump) / SKIP_DUPLICATE / MANUAL_REVIEW). Stay/lifecycle resolution lives in the engine, never the connector; the connector hint is non-authoritative. Idempotency-first (already-applied identity -> SKIP_DUPLICATE); ambiguous/unknown transitions fail closed to MANUAL_REVIEW with bounded machine review codes; no financial command / no Posting. Unit-tested (GI/GC/GO x state matrix, idempotency, determinism, bounded review codes); build/vet/test green. INCREMENT 4 TRANSACTIONAL PROCESSOR DONE + race-green + PG16-green: stayengine.Processor.ProcessNext consumes ONE pending CONSUMABLE inbox row (LIVE, or RESYNC whose resync_generation is published) in a single transaction -- FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED-claims the event, FOR UPDATE-locks the reservation Stay, Resolves, applies the authoritative Op, and moves the event PENDING->terminal exactly once (guarded by processing_status=PENDING; the migration trigger enforces the terminal-result rules). CREATE_STAY inserts the Stay (IN_HOUSE, lifecycle_version 1) + primary Guest + Folio IDENTITY record (no financial state); UPDATE_STAY refreshes names/dates/status; ROOM_MOVE updates the room on the same episode; CHECKOUT->CHECKED_OUT; REINSTATE->IN_HOUSE + EXACTLY one lifecycle_version bump; SKIP_DUPLICATE and MANUAL_REVIEW (bounded review code) apply no Stay mutation. One authoritative Stay per reservation per interface (external_stay_identity=reservation); lifecycle_version is the episode counter. PG16 end-to-end lifecycle proven: GI(create)->GC(update)->room-move->GO(checkout)->duplicate-GO(skip)->GI(reinstate to lv2); orphan GO->MANUAL_REVIEW with zero Stay; empty-inbox no-op. CI race + PG16 gates extended to internal/stayengine. REMAINING in Increment 4: Sharers (multi-guest), source-conflict handling, arrival/departure-correction refinements. INCREMENT 5 STARTED: new package data-plane/internal/pmsresolve with the STRICT multi-PMS resolver DECISION CORE (Resolve over the COMPLETE per-interface candidate vector derived from the trusted guest network, never client hints; never first/fastest). Precedence: unmapped/over-cap -> INDETERMINATE fail-closed; >=2 VERIFIED or any AMBIGUOUS_LOCAL -> AMBIGUOUS (discriminator escalation); any UNAVAILABLE/STALE/UNSUPPORTED_EVIDENCE -> INDETERMINATE fail-closed; exactly one VERIFIED (all others NO_MATCH) -> VERIFIED success; else NO_MATCH. Only a single-VERIFIED is guest-visible; every non-success is uniform with an internal-only bounded reason code. Unit-tested D1-D11 (namespace independence, ambiguity->discriminator, slow-VERIFIED-beats-fast-NO_MATCH, indeterminate-fail-closed, unmapped-fail-closed, candidate-cap, forged-hints-ignored, publish-rule, all-NO_MATCH, uniform-non-success); D9 sharers is a Stay-engine concern. CI race/gofmt/vet extended to internal/pmsresolve. REMAINING: the resolver DB wiring (derive candidates from guest_network_pms_map + auth_resolutions idempotency). INCREMENT 6 STARTED: new package data-plane/internal/authctx -- the one-time, TTL-bounded PMS AUTH CONTEXT. A successful STRICT resolution issues an Auth Context (NOT a session directly) with SERVER-DERIVED pins (tenant/site/interface/revision/stay from the resolution; device/guest-network from the trusted request); expires_at is computed server-side (now()+TTL) so no client clock influences the lifetime. Consume is a single-row atomic UPDATE ... WHERE consumed_at IS NULL AND expires_at>now(): it succeeds EXACTLY once (returning the server pins), and a replay or an expired context affects zero rows -> ErrContextInvalid (uniform, no detail). PG16-proven: issue->consume-once->replay-rejected->expired-rejected, and 16 concurrent consumers of one context yield EXACTLY ONE winner (no double-spend). CI gofmt/vet + disposable-PG16 integration extended to internal/authctx. AUTH CONTEXT CONSUMPTION EXTENDED (PO item 5) + PG16-green: Consume verifies the FULL server pin set -- tenant/site + device + guest-network + (for PMS) pinned Interface still ACTIVE + pinned Revision still present + Stay still IN_HOUSE + occupancy evidence present, not clock-suspect, matching the PINNED occupancy_evidence_version (new auth_contexts.pinned_occupancy_evidence_version, Migration 0010) and fresh under the Revision max_auth_cache_age (fail-closed 300s). AUTH-CONTEXT PROVENANCE + ISSUANCE VALIDATION (PO corrections) + PG16-green: ConsumeTx now also binds the Stay occupancy evidence to the EXACT immutable Revision the context authenticated against (st.occupancy_revision_id = ac.authentication_interface_revision_id) -- a matching evidence-version integer under a DIFFERENT Revision is rejected; a newer published Revision does not by itself invalidate the pinned one. The freshness parse is regex-guarded + LEAST-capped (max 7 days) with a strict 300s fail-closed default, so a malformed/non-numeric/zero/negative/out-of-range max_auth_cache_age can never cause a cast error or an unbounded window. IssuePMS fails BEFORE any INSERT (typed sanitized ErrGrantIncomplete, no identifier leak) when any required server pin is missing/invalid: tenant/site/interface/revision/stay/device/guest-network non-empty, positive occupancy-evidence version, positive bounded TTL (<= 3600) -- never persisting an unusable or already-expired context. PG16 tests: exact-revision-succeeds, different-occupancy-revision-rejected, newer-revision-does-not-invalidate, incomplete-issuance-zero-rows (zero/neg version, zero/neg TTL, each missing id), malformed/out-of-range freshness fail-closed. STATUS: Increment 3 SOFTWARE COMPLETE (live evidence pending); Increments 4-7 core/integration in progress; Phase 3 IMPLEMENTATION IN PROGRESS, not accepted/closed. EPISODE + EVIDENCE-SNAPSHOT PIN (PO corrections) + PG16-green + lifecycle-gate 121/121: auth_contexts now pins the authoritative Stay EPISODE (pinned_lifecycle_version) + a MONOTONIC occupancy-evidence snapshot (stays.occupancy_evidence_version, DISTINCT from the parser/normalization version; both new in Migration 0010) -- not a timestamp alone. IssuePMSTx derives lifecycle_version + occupancy_evidence_version ATOMICALLY from the LOCKED resolved Stay row (the caller supplies only the verified resolution identity, never guesses the counters); OccupancyEvidenceVersion removed from the caller grant. ConsumeTx rejects if the episode or evidence snapshot changed, so a Checkout->Reinstatement (lifecycle_version bump) or an authoritative evidence replacement invalidates an old context even within TTL. Freshness parse made CAST-SAFE + strictly bounded: the regex limits to 1-6 digits (the ::int can never overflow) inside a NESTED CASE, and a value > 604800 / malformed / zero / negative / overflow-sized -> strict 300s default (NEVER widened to 7 days). PG16 tests: checkout->reinstate-rejects-old + new-episode-succeeds, evidence-replacement-rejects, unchanged-succeeds, published-newer-revision-does-not-invalidate, freshness fail-closed for abc/0/-5/604801/2147483648/20-digit-overflow + honored-300 + honored-604800, incomplete-issuance-zero-rows. AUTH-CONTEXT LOCK-ORDER + EVIDENCE-VERSION ENFORCEMENT + PIN VALIDATION (PO corrections) + PG16-green + lifecycle-gate 131/131: (1) stays.occupancy_evidence_version monotonicity is now STRUCTURALLY enforced, not comment-only -- a stays_evidence_version_coherent CHECK (evidence present <=> version>0, absent <=> version=0) plus the p3_stay_lifecycle_guard trigger (fixed search_path, no SECURITY DEFINER, PUBLIC EXECUTE revoked): version never decreases, a MATERIAL evidence change (evidence_at/revision_id/normalization_version/clock_suspect; ingested_at is metadata, excluded) increments by EXACTLY 1, an unchanged material tuple must leave the version unchanged (arbitrary jump / bump-without-change rejected), and no caller can mutate evidence without the required transition; IssuePMSTx additionally rejects occupancy_evidence_version<=0. Lifecycle-gate proves evidence-present-version-0 rejected, decrease rejected, arbitrary jump rejected, mutation-without-transition rejected, 0->1 and N->N+1 valid, duplicate reapplication (ingested_at refresh only) keeps the version. (2) ConsumeTx now obeys the global L1 Stay-first lock order: it resolves the context's scoped Stay identity, LOCKS the Stay row FIRST (SELECT..FOR UPDATE OF st) while re-verifying the full pin set, and only THEN atomically flips the Auth Context unconsumed->consumed (one concurrent winner) -- it never locks the context before the Stay, so it serializes deterministically against Checkout/Reinstatement/evidence-replacement with no deadlock. New PG16 concurrency tests: Checkout-locks-first->consume-waits-then-rejects, consume-locks-first->Checkout-waits-until-commit, evidence-replacement-locks-first->old-context-rejects, failed-commerce-rolls-back-consumption, >=24 mixed Checkout/Consume no-deadlock, plus the isolated PROVENANCE clause proven via a trigger-bypassed same-version/different-Revision state. (3) Every required pin + presenter id is validated as a proper non-nil UUID in Go BEFORE any SQL (typed ErrGrantIncomplete/ErrContextInvalid, never a raw PostgreSQL cast error, zero inserted/updated rows on malformed/whitespace/overlong/nil input, no supplied identifier in the error text). (4) IssuePMSTx revalidates the pinned Revision freshness at issuance so an already-stale/clock-suspect Stay never yields a persisted context. (5) StayGuest pinning remains DEFERRED to the Sharers (Increment 4/5) wiring -- the Auth Context is therefore NOT yet described as having the complete final pin set. STATUS synced: phases[3].maturity + increment_status = Increment 3 SOFTWARE COMPLETE (live evidence pending), Increments 4-7 integration in progress, Increment 8/9 NOT STARTED; a replay, expiry, wrong device, wrong guest-network, cross-scope, or no-longer-valid occupancy all return the uniform ErrContextInvalid. ConsumeTx runs the one-time consumption INSIDE the caller transaction so it commits/rolls back ATOMICALLY with the Quote/Purchase -- a failed commerce transaction leaves the context UNCONSUMED (PG16-proven rollback), never permanently burned. PG16 tests: wrong-device/wrong-network rejected then correct presenter still consumes, checked-out-stay rejected, atomic-rollback-keeps-consumable, plus the prior one-time/replay/expiry/concurrent-single-winner. REMAINING in Increment 6: Phase-2 Commerce eligibility (EligibilitySubject from the pinned Auth Context Stay fields; capability-disabled Stay-based rule types; fail-closed on absent Stay evidence); then Increment 7. INCREMENT 7 (CORRECTED PO GRACE SEMANTICS) core DONE + green: package data-plane/internal/grace. CHECKOUT GRACE ELIGIBILITY = every checked-out Stay with an ACTIVE VALID Entitlement at effective_checkout_at gets EXACTLY ONE Grace conversion per lifecycle episode, REGARDLESS of the prior package origin/price (FREE/PAID/Stay-based/other); device activity is NOT the test; grace_duration_seconds is the Grace LIFETIME, not eligibility; no active entitlement -> no Grace; duplicate episode -> no duplicate. EMERGENCY GRACE = the fallback when the Hotel-Admin-configured Checkout Grace policy is invalid/unavailable at the ATOMIC conversion (versioned built-in 60m/5Mbps/2Mbps/500MB/REJECT_NEW_DEVICE; trigger EMERGENCY_GRACE + is_emergency + CHECKOUT_GRACE_CONFIG_INVALID alert) -- NOT a cloud-outage reauthorization (the CloudOutage/OutageAgeSeconds branch is REMOVED; local-first outage merely keeps enforcing already-authoritative state, never mints a new entitlement for a Guest whose access expired). ValidatePolicy is the SERVER-SIDE boundary (disabled/no-pinned-revision/malformed -> invalid). DecideAccess grandfathers existing devices within the window, rejects EVERY new post-checkout device under REJECT_NEW_DEVICE (below the grace device limit does NOT admit a new device; ExistingDevicesOverLimit is admin-visibility only), grandfathers existing devices even above the limit, applies grace shaping. Unit-tested: origin-agnostic eligibility (FREE/PAID/Stay-based same result), zero-usage-still-eligible, no-entitlement->no-grace, duplicate-episode-no-dup, pinned Admin policy + duration, config-invalid->emergency-built-in policy values, server-side validation, access grandfather/window/limit, determinism. F1 Room-Move + F7 episode idempotency live in the Stay engine. REMAINING in Increment 7 (DB layer, PO items 3/4): Hotel-Admin grace-policy CRUD + server validation + pinned-at-conversion; the atomic Checkout conversion (end the original entitlement at the boundary, create ONE Grace entitlement/purchase with one-conversion-per-episode uniqueness + concurrent-single-winner, rebind sessions atomically without logout/redirect/captive-portal, grandfather devices, grace shaping via tc without nft churn, quota/time counters from the boundary, idempotent duplicate accounting). Then Increment 8 (the two UIs) and Increment 9 tooling. REMAINING: Increments 4-9 (4 Stay/Event/Guest/Folio engine consuming the durable inbox; 5 STRICT multi-PMS resolver D1-D11; 6 PMS Auth Context + Phase-2 Commerce; 7 Room Move/Checkout Grace/Emergency Grace/Reinstatement F1-F7; 8 Guest Portal + Hotel Admin UIs; 9 deployment/live-verification/rollback/reboot/evidence tooling + runbooks, live execution deferred to the Product-Owner/operator). Increments 4-9 REMAIN (Increment 4 Stay/Event/Sharer/Folio engine; 5 STRICT multi-PMS resolver D1-D11; 6 PMS Auth Context + Phase-2 Commerce; 7 Room Move/Checkout Grace/Emergency Grace/Reinstatement F1-F7; 8 Guest Portal + Hotel Admin; 9 controlled live read-only PMS verification + live-dark deployment + reboot + final evidence). Phase 3 NOT accepted/closed; all flags OFF; no appliance/Production/live-PMS contact; awaiting Increments 4-9 + one final Product-Owner acceptance decision. PRIOR increment-3 skeleton (advanced/testable, superseded by Part B): data-plane/cmd/pmsd + internal/pmsd (Deps-injected Run, supervisor one-worker-per-interface, worker ordered ownership lock->re-read->startup-persist->dial->serve, single-owner advisory Locker + pgLocker, typed Repo + pgRepo with optimistic runtime_generation upsert, three-axis freshness persistence, FIAS outbound allowlist rejecting PS/PA reusing internal/pms -- no second stack, sanitized bounded error codes) + deploy/systemd/stayconnect-pmsd.service (least-privilege: dedicated user, NoNewPrivileges, empty caps, PrivateTmp/ProtectHome/ProtectSystem=strict, Restart=on-failure so DARK never storms); flags-OFF => zero DB/repo/locker/socket/worker (spy-proven); go build/vet/gofmt clean + go test -race PASS on Linux (flags-off zero-side-effect, fail-closed, one/two-worker, competing-owner-no-socket, disabled-no-dial, revision-reread, lock-loss-closes-socket, stale-generation-rejected, startup-no-fake-HEALTHY, outbound-no-PS, sanitize-no-PII, backoff-bounded, SIGTERM-no-goroutine-leak, one-failure-isolation, lock-key-deterministic). No live PMS connection (dark; live dial deferred to increment 9). NOT YET DONE (increments 4-8): Stay/Event/Folio engine; STRICT resolver; PMS Auth Context; Commerce integration; Room Move; Checkout Grace + emergency fallback; Reinstatement; Guest Portal + Hotel Admin UI; D1-D11/F1-F7 tests; and (increment 9) live READ-ONLY PMS verification; live-dark deployment + reboot; final acceptance report. All Phase-3 flags OFF; no PMS connection; zero Phase-3 SQL in production; no runtime iam_v2 grants; no PMS financial posting; no live guest use; no Production DB accessed (all schema work on a disposable container).

3.title: PMS Stay Domain, STRICT Multi-PMS Resolution, Room Movement, Checkout Grace and Reinstatement
3.maturity: IMPLEMENTATION IN PROGRESS as one end-to-end Phase (D14, start transition T0015), DARK. Increment 1 (governance + planning) and Increment 2 (migration 0010 hardened + pms_config flags + machine-grounded gap audit; lifecycle gate 121/121 on disposable PG16) DELIVERED + CI-green. Increment 3 (dedicated read-only PMS connector runtime, pmsd) SOFTWARE IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE + CI-green: strict per-frame validation, single serialized protocol writer, durable DS->DE resync inbox reusing stay_events with typed monotonic resync generations + an atomic single-row publication boundary, full application barrier, durable ownership-safe append-first admission (ownership-proof + runtime-generation CAS), credential_mode NONE + owner-bound AES-GCM AAD, assignment reuse/rotation, lock-loss, panic recovery, startup validation -- race-green (Docker golang:1.25) + PG16-green. Live Increment-9 READ-ONLY PMS verification evidence is PENDING (hardware step, deferred to the Product-Owner/operator). Increments 4-7 INTEGRATION IN PROGRESS -- decision cores + durable persistence delivered green (race + disposable PG16): Increment 4 Stay resolution engine + transactional inbox processor (episodes via lifecycle_version; PG16 lifecycle); Increment 5 STRICT multi-PMS resolver decision core (D1-D11); Increment 6 one-time PMS Auth Context with full server-pin + episode + monotonic occupancy-evidence-snapshot verification, STRUCTURALLY-enforced evidence-version monotonicity (coherence CHECK + lifecycle trigger), Go-side non-nil-UUID pin/presenter validation, issuance-time freshness revalidation, and an L1 Stay-first, deadlock-free atomic consume-with-purchase (lifecycle gate 131/131); Increment 7 Checkout + Emergency Grace decision cores (origin-agnostic eligibility, REJECT_NEW_DEVICE, config-invalid emergency fallback). The Auth Context StayGuest (Sharer) pin remains deferred to the Increment 4/5 Sharers wiring, so it is not yet the complete final pin set. Remaining backend wiring: atomic Checkout conversion, Sharers/source-conflict, resolver candidate fan-out + auth_resolutions idempotency, Commerce eligibility + full disposable flow, F1-F7 DB. Increment 8 (Guest Portal + Hotel Admin UIs) and Increment 9 (offline deployment/verification/rollback/evidence tooling + runbooks) NOT STARTED. All Phase-3 flags OFF; migration 0010 NOT applied to the appliance or Production; live PMS verification is READ-ONLY to a disposable test DB; no financial posting, no PS sender, no IAM-v2 cutover, no Phase 4+. Phase 3 NOT accepted/closed; awaiting complete execution + one final Product-Owner acceptance decision.

database_schema_state.public_structural_fingerprint_phase1a_md: MD5 of sorted public-schema {columns + constraints + indexes} excluding the Phase-1A MG-0 anchor index guest_networks_tsi_anchor (iam_v2_scratch/prod_live_dark.sh). Phase-1A baseline; PRE-Phase-1B / pre-0007-0008.
database_schema_state.public_columns_sha256_algorithm: Phase-1B/Gate-P value. SHA-256 over information_schema.columns table.column:data_type, computed by the Gate-P tooling (iam_v2_scratch/prod_live_dark.sh). Distinct algorithm from the Phase-1A MD5 above; NOT comparable to it.
database_schema_state.public_columns_phase2_livedark_algorithm: Phase-2 live-dark value. encode(digest(string_agg(table_name||'.'||column_name||':'||data_type, chr(10) ORDER BY 1),'sha256'),'hex') over information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='public', computed read-only on 172.21.60.23 during the Phase-2 live-dark deploy.
database_schema_state.public_columns_fingerprint_reconciliatio: public_columns_sha256_current (7241b1cd, Phase-1B/Gate-P) and public_columns_phase2_livedark_sha256 (833c3d67, Phase-2 live-dark) use DIFFERENT serializations (per-object Gate-P method vs aggregate string_agg+newline+digest). They were NOT re-verified against each other on the appliance (this reconciliation does not touch the appliance), so they are recorded as SEPARATE, NOT-directly-comparable fields. The Phase-2 darkness invariant is that the 833c3d67 value is IDENTICAL pre- and post-migration-0009 (public schema unchanged by 0009) -- proven in docs/evidence/StayConnect-IAM-Phase2-Live-Dark-Evidence.md -- not equality between the two named fields.
database_schema_state.public_structure_change_note: The Phase-1A structural baseline was intentionally superseded by the AUTHORIZED additive migrations 0007/0008 (auth_throttle_buckets, otp_hmac_key_generations, auth_otps.otp_key_generation). The pre->post SHA-256 delta (8659c08d -> 7241b1cd) is exactly those additive objects; this is authorized schema evolution, not drift.
database_schema_state.migration_0009_note: Phase-2 additive iam_v2 migration 0009_phase2_commerce applied live on 172.21.60.23 (2 trigger functions + 2 triggers + 6 lookup indexes, owned by iam_v2_owner). The public-schema columns SHA-256 is unchanged pre->post (public schema untouched); iam_v2 remains 0 rows.
